top_categories.workout_context: 5,413ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Sellaronda Bike Day's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
33 miles on paved Dolomite mountain pass roads burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Sellaronda Bike Day's paved Dolomite mountain pass roads forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 33 miles.
Base fitness for Sellaronda Bike Day. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,413ft of climbing on paved Dolomite mountain pass roads does to your power file on race day.
164ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Sellaronda Bike Day. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Dolomite mountain pass roads. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
At Sellaronda Bike Day, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Sellaronda Bike Day, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Standard hydration and fueling. One bottle per hour minimum. Gel or bar every 45 minutes at race intensity.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Sellaronda Bike Day. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 33 miles of paved dolomite mountain pass roads in Sella Group, Dolomites, Italy.
